| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Truly I say to you, He who does not go through the door into the place where the sheep are kept, but gets in by some other way, is a thief and an outlaw. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | He who goes in by the door is the keeper of the sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | The porter lets him in; and the sheep give ear to his voice; he says over the names of the sheep, and takes them out. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | When he has got them all out, he goes before them, and the sheep go after him, for they have knowledge of his voice. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | They will not go after another who is not their keeper, but will go from him in flight, because his voice is strange to them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | In this Jesus was teaching them in the form of a story: but what he said was not clear to them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | So Jesus said again, Truly I say to you, I am the door of the sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | All who came before me are thieves and outlaws: but the sheep did not give ear to them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | I am the door: if any man goes in through me he will have salvation, and will go in and go out, and will get food. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | The thief comes only to take the sheep and to put them to death: he comes for their destruction: I have come so that they may have life and have it in greater measure. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | I am the good keeper of sheep: the good keeper gives his life for the sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | He who is a servant, and not the keeper or the owner of the sheep, sees the wolf coming and goes in flight, away from the sheep; and the wolf comes down on them and sends them in all directions: | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | Because he is a servant he has no interest in the sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | I am the good keeper; I have knowledge of my sheep, and they have knowledge of me, | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Even as the Father has knowledge of me and I of the Father; and I am giving my life for the sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | And I have other sheep which are not of this field: I will be their guide in the same way, and they will give ear to my voice, so there will be one flock and one keeper. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | For this reason am I loved by the Father, because I give up my life so that I may take it again. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | No one takes it away from me; I give it up of myself. I have power to give it up, and I have power to take it again. These orders I have from my Father. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | There was a division again among the Jews because of these words. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | And a number of them said, He has an evil spirit and is out of his mind; why do you give ear to him? | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | Others said, These are not the words of one who has an evil spirit. Is it possible for an evil spirit to make blind people see? | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Then came the feast of the opening of the Temple in Jerusalem: it was winter; | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | And Jesus was walking in the Temple, in Solomon's covered way. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Then the Jews came round him, saying, how long are you going to keep us in doubt? If you are the Christ, say so clearly. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | Jesus said in answer, I have said it and you have no belief: the works which I do in my Father's name, these give witness about me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | But you have no belief because you are not of my sheep. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | My sheep give ear to my voice, and I have knowledge of them, and they come after me: | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | And I give them eternal life; they will never come to destruction, and no one will ever take them out of my hand. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | That which my Father has given to me has more value than all; and no one is able to take anything out of the Father's hand. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | I and my Father are one. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | Then the Jews took up stones again to send at him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
| [32](BBE.md#v32) | Jesus said to them in answer, I have let you see a number of good works from the Father; for which of those works are you stoning me? | [WEB](WEB.md#v32) · [KJV](KJV.md#v32) · [ASV](ASV.md#v32) |
| [33](BBE.md#v33) | This was their answer: We are not stoning you for a good work but for evil words; because being a man you make yourself God. | [WEB](WEB.md#v33) · [KJV](KJV.md#v33) · [ASV](ASV.md#v33) |
| [34](BBE.md#v34) | In answer, Jesus said, Is there not a saying in your law, I said, You are gods? | [WEB](WEB.md#v34) · [KJV](KJV.md#v34) · [ASV](ASV.md#v34) |
| [35](BBE.md#v35) | If he said they were gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Writings may not be broken), | [WEB](WEB.md#v35) · [KJV](KJV.md#v35) · [ASV](ASV.md#v35) |
| [36](BBE.md#v36) | Do you say of him whom the Father made holy and sent into the world, Your words are evil; because I said, I am God's Son? | [WEB](WEB.md#v36) · [KJV](KJV.md#v36) · [ASV](ASV.md#v36) |
| [37](BBE.md#v37) | If I am not doing the works of my Father, do not have belief in me; | [WEB](WEB.md#v37) · [KJV](KJV.md#v37) · [ASV](ASV.md#v37) |
| [38](BBE.md#v38) | But if I am doing them, then have belief in the works even if you have no belief in me; so that you may see clearly and be certain that the Father is in me and I am in the Father. | [WEB](WEB.md#v38) · [KJV](KJV.md#v38) · [ASV](ASV.md#v38) |
| [39](BBE.md#v39) | Then again they made an attempt to take him; but he got away from them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v39) · [KJV](KJV.md#v39) · [ASV](ASV.md#v39) |
| [40](BBE.md#v40) | And he went again to the other side of the Jordan, to the place where John first gave baptism; and he was there for a time. | [WEB](WEB.md#v40) · [KJV](KJV.md#v40) · [ASV](ASV.md#v40) |
| [41](BBE.md#v41) | And a great number of people came to him, saying, John did no sign: but everything John said of this man was true. | [WEB](WEB.md#v41) · [KJV](KJV.md#v41) · [ASV](ASV.md#v41) |
| [42](BBE.md#v42) | And a number came to have faith in him there. | [WEB](WEB.md#v42) · [KJV](KJV.md#v42) · [ASV](ASV.md#v42) |
